content:
FINAL TASK — RESTORE EXPO/REACT NATIVE EMULATOR ACCESS END TO END

My real application is an Expo/React Native application. I do not authorize replacing it with a Swift shell, and I do not accept Expo Go as the only preview method.

Repository:
@[github:https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fibb142%2Frork-global-real-estate-invest:GitHub Sync - ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest]

Expo application directory:
/expo

OBJECTIVE:
Make my existing Expo/React Native application the active Rork project and display the real app inside the Rork emulator for both Android and iOS.

EXECUTE NOW:

1. Audit the current workspace and confirm why it was initialized as Swift instead of React Native.

2. Preserve the complete existing Expo application, including:
   - all screens
   - Expo Router routes
   - owner login
   - authentication
   - API configuration
   - environment-variable names
   - assets
   - navigation
   - Supabase integration
   - Render backend integration
   - Android configuration
   - iOS configuration

3. Create a new React Native/Expo-compatible Rork project or re-register the current project correctly as React Native.

4. Import the existing `/expo` application into the React Native project. Do not rebuild a blank application and do not replace production code with placeholders.

5. Configure the React Native project root correctly. If `/expo` must become the project root, perform the repository restructuring safely and update:
   - package.json
   - app.json or app.config
   - eas.json
   - tsconfig.json
   - babel configuration
   - metro configuration
   - Expo Router entry
   - Android package configuration
   - iOS bundle configuration

6. Install all dependencies using the lockfile and resolve every dependency or SDK incompatibility.

7. Verify compatibility with the currently supported Expo SDK. Do not only state that SDK 54 is supported; run the actual checks and builds.

8. Start the Expo development server and register the application with Rork’s emulator preview system.

9. Launch and verify:
   - Android emulator
   - iOS simulator/emulator where supported
   - web preview
   - Expo Go QR preview

10. Confirm that the emulator opens the real IVX application, not a Swift placeholder, blank screen, demo screen, or shell.

11. Test these routes and functions in the emulator:
   - landing page
   - member registration
   - owner sign-in
   - owner dashboard
   - IVX IA chat
   - navigation between screens
   - Render API connection
   - Supabase connection

12. Fix all red screens, Metro errors, missing modules, route errors, environment-binding errors, and runtime crashes.

13. Commit and push all required changes to:
   ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest

14. Do not move ownership of the source code to a Rork-controlled repository. My GitHub repository must remain the source of truth.

REQUIRED PROOF:

A. GitHub repository and branch
B. Final commit SHA
C. Exact project root used by Rork
D. package.json Expo and React Native versions
E. Dependency installation result
F. Expo diagnostics result
G. Android emulator screenshot showing the real IVX app
H. iOS emulator screenshot showing the real IVX app, if supported
I. Web preview URL and HTTP result
J. Metro terminal output showing successful bundle
K. Owner sign-in test result
L. Render API health result
M. Supabase connection result
N. List of every file changed
O. Final status using exactly one:
   - VERIFIED_COMPLETE
   - BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_PLATFORM_ERROR

IMPORTANT:
Do not answer with instructions telling me to clone the repository on another computer.
Do not ask me to manually create another project.
Do not ask for credentials unless a real test returns missing, expired, revoked, 401, or 403.
Do not stop after creating the project.
Complete the React Native import, emulator registration, testing, fixes, GitHub push, and proof end to end now.
